# UC-23.6.5 · Purchase Order Cycle Time and Maverick Spend

## Description

This monitoring posture stitches heterogeneous procure-to-pay snapshots — Coupa REST extracts, SAP Ariba Open API plus cXML invoice envelopes, Oracle Fusion Procurement REST projections, SAP S/4HANA Materials Management tables (`EKKO`, `EKPO`, `EBAN`), Workday Procurement Reports-as-a-Service bundles, Dynamics 365 Finance package feeds — into one Splunk timeline where purchase identifiers (`po_id`, `po_number`, supplier keys, commodity categories, legal entities, cost centers) collapse through `coalesce()` chains and explicit `source_system` stamps (`coupa`, `ariba`, `oracle_proc`, `sap_mm`, `workday_proc`, `dynamics365`). Each purchase flows requested timestamps through approval, goods receipt or substitute milestones, invoice receipt, and settlement markers so Splunk can derive calendar-day deltas (`requisition_to_approval_days`, `requisition_to_first_receipt_days`, `po_to_invoice_days`, `po_to_payment_days`) optionally shaded against `business_calendar.csv` holidays before percentile ranks slice procurement queues by organizational facets.

## Value

Chief Procurement Officers and finance controllers defend negotiated discounts referenced in CAPS Research maverick-spend definitions and APQC Procurement benchmarks — ROSPA wording ties maverick purchasing to purchases lacking aligned contracts or routed outside preferred catalogs — while treasury peers chase Coupa BSM Index-style approval-cycle reductions versus Hackett Group Procurement Performance Study cycle-time quartiles (median purchase-order cycles near nine calendar days versus four-day exemplars). Rolling Splunk arithmetic exposes leakage modeled through `savings_leakage_usd`, aligns invoice automation ratios drawn from Coupa AP Automation or SAP MIRO touchless postings against targets approaching eighty-percent straight-through processing on catalog-heavy catalogs, pairs EcoVadis or Dun & Bradstreet supplier-risk overlays with commodity-category concentration charts so sanctions-review queues inherit objective prioritization, and hands audit committees tangible drill paths parallel to vendor-native dashboards such as Coupa Spend Analysis tiles, SAP Ariba Spend Visibility workbooks, Oracle Procurement dashboards, Workday Procurement operational packs — distinct from accounts-receivable aging (UC-23.6.1), expense-report anomalies (UC-23.6.2), budget-variance scrutiny (UC-23.6.3), or payment-gateway authorization telemetry (UC-23.6.4). Manufacturing plus Energy-intensive sectors exhibit heavy direct-material bills-of-material urgency; Healthcare ordering blends formulary-driven catalogs with urgent clinical buys; Public-sector solicitations stretch statutory thresholds; Retail seasonal ramps accent punch-out catalogs — those industries concentrate procurement staffing pressure Splunk dashboards relieve earliest.

## Implementation

(1) Publish JDBC projections or REST pollers feeding normalized sourcetypes (`coupa:po_rest`, `dbx:sap_mm_po`, etc.) into `index=business`, stamping `_time` from upstream change timestamps rather than indexer lag alone; clone Splunk Add-on for REST stanza referencing OAuth vault secrets per tenant; mirror SAP Ariba cXML invoice flows through middleware straight into HEC with retained signatures for forensic replay; ingest Dynamics exports via Splunk Add-on for AWS S3 with KMS-backed buckets where Finance mandates encryption-at-rest parity; replicate invoice-match telemetry (`dbx:invoice_match_log`) plus sanctions screenings (`supplier_risk.csv`). (2) Upload governance lookups (`preferred_suppliers.csv`, `active_contracts.csv`, `business_calendar.csv`, `workflow_allowlist.csv`, `budget_bridge_qtd.csv`) through Lookup Editor with ACLs restricted to Procurement Master Data plus Treasury controllers; refresh EcoVadis or Dun & Bradstreet extracts weekly; bind Coupa Verified Suppliers identifiers through deterministic keys (`supplier_id`). (3) Paste composite SPL into macro-parameterized saved searches (`macro_proc_cycle_window`, `macro_maverick_pct_floor`) so thresholds evolve without rewriting forty dashboards; accelerate summary indexing optional once cardinality stabilizes; throttle Slack / Teams alerts via two-week streak detectors (`streamstats`) evaluating maverick percentages per commodity category. (4) Route alerts through Procurement Operations Manager channels plus ServiceNow orchestrated incidents referencing Splunk drill URLs while respecting segregation-of-duties naming conventions Finance publishes.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud aligned with splunkVersions; Heavy Forwarder clusters permitted outbound HTTPS toward Coupa tenants (`*.coupa.com`), SAP Ariba realms (`*.ariba.com`), Oracle Fusion pods (`*.oraclecloud.com`), Microsoft Dynamics Lifecycle Services endpoints, plus JDBC lanes into SAP HANA (`jdbc:sap://host:30015`) or Oracle ATP describing Fusion schemas hosting `PO_HEADERS_ALL`/`PO_LINES_ALL`/`PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL`/`PO_REQUISITIONS_ALL`/`PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ALL`.
• Secrets vault storing OAuth client identifiers for Splunk Add-on for REST poll loops plus rotating webhook secrets protecting SAP Ariba cXML ingest relays.
• Splunk roles restricting indexed procurement payloads (purchase dollar totals, hashed identities, commodity mixes) to Procurement Operations, Strategic Sourcing, Finance FP&A partners named inside RACI matrices — deny broader audiences masked-hash columns remain reversible only inside Identity Governance tooling.
• Governance lookups governed jointly by Procurement Master Data and Treasury Policy (`preferred_suppliers.csv`, `active_contracts.csv`, `business_calendar.csv`, `workflow_allowlist.csv`, `budget_bridge_qtd.csv`, `supplier_risk.csv`, `emergency_authorization_register.csv`, `pilot_program_supplier_register.csv`) version-controlled outside Splunk yet promoted nightly.

Step 1 — Configure multi-source ingestion and parsing contracts
Path A — Coupa REST via Splunk Add-on for REST (Splunkbase 1546): define modular inputs targeting `/api/purchase_orders` plus `/api/suppliers`, OAuth client-credentials tokens refreshed hourly, interval sixty minutes default, sourcetype `coupa:po_rest`. Ship JSON untouched then normalize fields via transforms.conf FIELDALIAS directives mapping vendor payloads toward canonical keys (`requested_at`, `approved_at`, `amount`). Sample upstream vendor JSON excerpt referenced during design reviews stays illustrative:
{"id":12345,"po-number":"PO-2024-001","supplier":{"id":"s_123","name":"Acme Corp"},"requested-by":"john.doe@company.com","approved-at":"2024-09-15T14:22:00Z","payment-terms":{"name":"Net 30"},"order-lines":[{"line-num":1,"sku":"SKU-123","qty":10,"unit-price":150.00,"commodity-code":"6010"}]}
Index-time directives belong on indexers / heavy forwarders hosting parsing tier:
[coupa:po_rest]
KV_MODE = json
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TIME_PREFIX = "approved-at":"|"requested-at":"|"created-at":"|"modified-at":"
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TRUNCATE = 1000000
TIMESTAMP_FIELDS = approved-at,created-at,modified-at
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z

Path B — SAP Ariba Open APIs plus cXML invoice envelopes: middleware verifies HMAC-SHA256 signatures on inbound HTTPS posts before Splunk HTTP Event Collector accepts payloads into `index=business` sourcetype `ariba:cxml_invoice`. Maintain replay queues referencing SAP documentation on `<Order>` / `<ConfirmationRequest>` payloads.

Path C — Oracle Procurement Cloud JDBC via Splunk DB Connect batched selects joining `PO_HEADERS_ALL`, `PO_LINES_ALL`, `PO_DISTRIBUTIONS_ALL`, `PO_REQUISITIONS_ALL`, `PO_LINE_LOCATIONS_ALL`, projecting `_time` from `LAST_UPDATE_DATE`. Parallel REST incremental pulls optional via Fusion ICS invoking `/fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.18.05/purchaseOrders`.

Path D — SAP S/4HANA Materials Management via DB Connect extracts referencing `EKKO`, `EKPO`, `EBAN`, plus goods-movement contexts (`EKBE`) feeding derived `first_receipt_at`; alternatively OData poll `API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV` PurchaseOrder entities mirroring ME21N/ME22N/ME23N semantics.

Path E — Workday Procurement via Reports-as-a-Service delivering XML snapshots onto SFTP shares Splunk monitors via scripted inputs landing sourcetype `workday:procurement_raas`.

Path F — Dynamics 365 Finance package drops exported through Data Management Framework into Azure Blob / AWS S3 buckets harvested through Splunk Add-on for AWS S3 with KMS SSE-KMS encryption parity Finance mandates.

Publish consolidated FIELDALIAS bundles aligning feeds toward canonical identifiers (`po_id`, `po_number`, `requisition_id`, `requester_email_hash`, `approver_email_hash`, `supplier_id`, `supplier_name`, `requested_at`, `approved_at`, `first_receipt_at`, `invoice_received_at`, `paid_at`, `po_amount_usd`, `commodity_category`, `cost_center`, `gl_account`, `legal_entity`, `source_system`). Hash surrogate columns originate inside Identity tooling feeding nightly CSV lookups rather than reversible Splunk macros.

Augment ingestion with supplemental lookups tying Contract Lifecycle Management feeds (`active_contracts.csv` sourced from Icertis Contract Intelligence, Ironclad CLM, DocuSign CLM nightly snapshots) plus procurement-card overlays (`pcard_transactions.csv`) isolating spend bypassing PO workflows.

Stand Splunk KV Store collections (`kv_procurement_exceptions`) capturing reviewer disposition codes (`confirmed_maestro_exception`, `needs_catalog_refresh`) surfaced via KV Store lookups optional.

Step 2 — Composite SPL with maverick tiers and invoice-match overlays
Paste canonical SPL saved inside macros `macro_proc_cycle_window` controlling ninety-day horizons plus join thresholds referencing invoice-match summaries landing sourcetype `dbx:invoice_match_log` modeling Coupa AP Automation, SAP MIRO straight-through postings, Ariba Auto-Match verdicts expressed via `match_type` enumerations (`auto_3way`, `manual_review`).

Maestro classification honors ROSPA plus CAPS Research framing plus APQC Maverick Spend Percent definitions — tiers encoded inside SPL mirror procurement-policy wording:
• Off-contract purchases lacking supplier-plus-commodity contract identifiers (`off_contract`).
• Preferred supplier engagements purchasing commodities absent negotiated catalogs (`off_catalog`).
• Threshold-split automation routing purchases beneath dollar thresholds without sourcing-event identifiers (`below_threshold_bypass`).
• Routing anomalies referencing workflow_allowlist mismatches (`workflow_bypass`).

Parallel branches compute percentile aggregates (`perc50`, `perc95`) across organizational facets (`cost_center`, `commodity_category`) feeding CFO narratives anchored against external benchmarks cited inside governance decks — indirect procurement versus direct procurement thresholds referenced manually rather than automated assertions.

Budget overlays reuse FP&A extracts (`budget_bridge_qtd.csv`) maintaining parity with UC-23.6.3 variance modelling without duplicating SAP universal-journal ingestion described there.

Step 3 — Validation pathways referencing vendor consoles
Weekly reconcile Splunk aggregates versus Coupa Spend Analysis dashboards focusing identical commodity filters plus identical fiscal calendars — tolerate rounding deltas smaller than procurement-policy rounding tables (`±0.5%`). Cross-check SAP Ariba Spend Visibility saved views filtering identical supplier identifiers (`supplier_id`). Compare Oracle Procurement dashboards verifying Fusion totals versus JDBC extracts feeding Splunk counts.

Quarterly reconcile Splunk ninety-fifth percentile approval durations versus Workday Procurement canned operational metrics exported via identical RaaS report IDs Splunk ingestion consumes.

Annually capture screenshots referencing Splunk dashboards beside SAP GUI counts drawn from ME23N displays tied to identical PO numbers validating Splunk retains deterministic traces auditors request.

Step 4 — Operational automation cadence
Procurement Operations Manager Slack / Microsoft Teams alert triggers fire when commodity-category maverick percentages exceed ten percent across two successive rolling fourteen-day windows computed via secondary scheduled searches chaining `streamstats` streak detectors distinct from nightly summaries.

ServiceNow Incident workflows instantiate whenever Splunk joins supplier-risk lookups revealing OFAC statuses tagged `confirmed_match`, attaching sanitized Splunk URLs referencing hashed surrogate identities.

Optional downstream Workflow orchestrations invoke Coupa REST Workflows API staging hooks suspending problematic PO identifiers surfaced via Splunk notable events once FP&A confirms budget overrun interplay referencing UC-23.6.3 outputs.

Weekly CSV snapshots emailed toward Chief Procurement Officer distribution lists summarize ranked savings leakage totals referencing Splunk macro outputs archived inside immutable proof stores satisfying audit retention schedules negotiated separately.

Step 5 — Misleading modes procurement analysts rehearse during quarterly readiness drills
• Authorized disaster-response buys flagged inside emergency_authorization_register.csv legitimately bypass sequential approvals yet Splunk tiers classify them as workflow anomalies unless contextual CSV joins annotate catastrophe chronologies referenced during tabletop exercises covering hurricanes.
• Capital-program engineering releases inflate measured modification frequencies versus indirect catalogs — excluding capital-project identifiers miscasts benign engineering churn as rogue procurement agility lacking governance storytelling controllers already documented.
• Fiscal fourth-quarter hurry-ups accelerate approvals compared with ordinary quarters — Splunk compresses perceived cycle durations despite deliberate controller sponsorship accelerating lawful backlog clears unrelated to procurement laziness narratives FP&A emphasizes externally.
• Startup incubator pilots sourcing vendors absent preferred-supplier registers intentionally produce catalog misses flagged off-contract until pilot_program_supplier_register.csv annotates incubator cohort tags Procurement sponsors openly.
• Functional-currency translations oscillate whenever treasury publishes ECB blended FX versus invoice-native currencies Coupa retains — Splunk monetary totals fluctuate absent operational deterioration whenever FX overlays stale nightly uploads treasury intentionally refreshes slower than transactional timestamps Splunk snapshots hourly.
• Seven-tier routing matrices elongate gigantic indirect buys exceeding hundred-thousand-dollar thresholds versus nimble catalog catalogs beneath thousand-dollar thresholds — naive Splunk summaries contrasting Marketing commodity averages versus Information Technology commodities omit normalization referencing routing tiers coded inside workflow_allowlist commentary Finance publishes privately.
• Manufacturing-requirement-planning cadences spawn SAP-managed automatic purchase orders skipping formal requisition approvals altogether — Splunk timelines shrink massively versus indirect buys although planners obey Materials Management governance referencing SAP transactions ME57 plus ME58 sequencing planners rehearse nightly.
• Punch-out catalogs referencing Amazon Business, Staples Advantage, MSC Industrial, Grainger integrations stamp instantaneous approvals reflecting negotiated catalogs finalized quarters earlier — Splunk cycle timers omit lengthy sourcing narratives preceding eventual clicks procurement historians archive offline.
• Duplicate supplier-master identifiers referencing Microsoft Corporation textual variants versus Microsoft Corp shorthand fracture totals across pseudo-distinct supplier IDs despite identical Dun & Bradstreet numbers until normalization pipelines reconcile duplicates nightly.
• Contract Lifecycle Management nightly extracts stall thirty-day intervals syncing renewal statuses — Splunk temporarily marks renewed contracts absent although procurement analysts track renewal paperwork separately referencing counsel-managed calendars Finance trusts implicitly.
• Professional-services procurements seldom capture goods receipts meaning logistics-invoice-verification ratios plummet artificially versus merchandise-heavy catalogs whenever Splunk insists triple-match percentages blanket-wide ignoring procurement-policy carve-outs documenting service exemptions planners cite verbally.
• Delegated approval reroutes stemming from vacation routing lengthen Splunk durations versus malicious procrastination whenever delegation matrices reroute workloads legitimately although Splunk lacks delegation telemetry absent supplementary Splunk ingestion referencing HR calendars procurement trusts selectively.
• Spot-buy sourcing overrides awarding purchases toward alternate vendors despite sourcing-event winners documented elsewhere — Splunk surfaces maverick classifications contradicted once sourcing-event journals annotate decisive waiver rationales reviewers archive separately.
• Bulk consolidated laboratory-supply baskets aggregate dozens of compliant micro-lines underneath aggregated weekly headers masking line-level residual maverick percentages Splunk aggregates flatten unintentionally obscuring microscopic anomalies auditors sampling statistically highlight sporadically.
• Procurement-card programs settling outside enterprise-resource-planning purchase-order workflows bypass Splunk numerator denominators whenever supplemental card extracts omit ingestion pipelines treasury insists stays segmented until quarterly audits reconcile consciously.
• Export-administration-controlled commodities reroute approvals toward specialized licensing desks lengthen Splunk durations resembling bureaucratic sluggishness despite lawful diligence honoring trade-compliance statutes regulators emphasize repeatedly.

## SPL

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="coupa:po_rest" OR sourcetype="ariba:cxml_invoice" OR sourcetype="oracle:fusion_po_rest" OR sourcetype="dbx:sap_mm_po" OR sourcetype="workday:procurement_raas" OR sourcetype="d365:purchase_orders") earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
| eval po_id=tostring(coalesce(po_id,PurchOrdId,PurchaseOrderId,FusionPoId,D365PoKey,"NOTSET"))
| eval po_number=tostring(coalesce(po_number,PONumber,PurchOrd,PurchasingDocument,ErpPoNum,""))
| eval supplier_id=tostring(coalesce(supplier_id,SupplierPartyId,VendorNumber,LIFNR,LFM1_VENDOR,"UNKNOWN"))
| eval supplier_name=tostring(coalesce(supplier_name,VendorName,SupplierLegalName,""))
| eval requested_at_raw=coalesce(requested_at,RequisitionSubmittedAt,CreatedDateUtc)
| eval approved_at_raw=coalesce(approved_at,FullyApprovedAt,OrderedDateUtc)
| eval first_receipt_at_raw=coalesce(first_receipt_at,FirstGoodsReceiptTs,GoodsReceiptDate)
| eval invoice_received_at_raw=coalesce(invoice_received_at,InvoiceSubmittedDate,MiroPostingDateHint)
| eval paid_at_raw=coalesce(paid_at,PaidAccountingDate,PaidTs)
| eval requested_epoch=coalesce(strptime(requested_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),strptime(requested_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3QZ"),strptime(requested_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval approved_epoch=coalesce(strptime(approved_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),strptime(approved_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3QZ"),strptime(approved_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval receipt_epoch=coalesce(strptime(first_receipt_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),strptime(first_receipt_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval invoice_epoch=coalesce(strptime(invoice_received_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),strptime(invoice_received_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval paid_epoch=coalesce(strptime(paid_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),strptime(paid_at_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval biz_requested=strftime(requested_epoch,"%Y-%m-%d")
| lookup business_calendar.csv date AS biz_requested OUTPUT holiday_flag AS holiday_req
| eval requisition_to_approval_days=if(isnotnull(requested_epoch) AND isnotnull(approved_epoch),round((approved_epoch-requested_epoch)/86400,2),null())
| eval requisition_to_first_receipt_days=if(isnotnull(requested_epoch) AND isnotnull(receipt_epoch),round((receipt_epoch-requested_epoch)/86400,2),null())
| eval po_to_invoice_days=if(isnotnull(approved_epoch) AND isnotnull(invoice_epoch),round((invoice_epoch-approved_epoch)/86400,2),null())
| eval po_to_payment_days=if(isnotnull(invoice_epoch) AND isnotnull(paid_epoch),round((paid_epoch-invoice_epoch)/86400,2),null())
| eval po_amount_usd=tonumber(coalesce(po_amount_usd,AmountUsd,PurchOrdAmount,TaxInclusiveAmt,"0"))
| eval commodity_category=tostring(coalesce(commodity_category,UNSPSC_Heading,CommodityCode,"misc"))
| eval cost_center=tostring(coalesce(cost_center,CostCenterCode,KOSTL_RESPONSIBLE,"UNK_CC"))
| eval legal_entity=tostring(coalesce(legal_entity,CompanyCode,LedgerEntity,"UNK_ENT"))
| eval source_system=case(
    sourcetype="coupa:po_rest","coupa",
    sourcetype="ariba:cxml_invoice","ariba",
    sourcetype="oracle:fusion_po_rest","oracle_proc",
    sourcetype="dbx:sap_mm_po","sap_mm",
    sourcetype="workday:procurement_raas","workday_proc",
    sourcetype="d365:purchase_orders","dynamics365",
    1=1,"unknown")
| eval po_line_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(po_line_cnt,NumberOfLines,"1"))
| eval complexity_score=po_line_cnt*if(po_amount_usd>250000,3,if(po_amount_usd>50000,2,1))
| lookup preferred_suppliers.csv supplier_id OUTPUT preferred_flag AS pref_csv, contract_id AS contract_pref, negotiated_discount_pct AS negotiated_discount_pct_csv
| lookup active_contracts.csv contract_id OUTPUT contract_expiry AS contract_expiry_csv, max_value AS contract_ceiling, commodity_categories_allowed AS cats_allowed_csv
| eval preferred_flag=coalesce(pref_csv,tostring(coalesce(IsPreferredVendor,TierOneSupplier,"false")))
| eval contract_id=coalesce(contract_pref,tostring(coalesce(MasterContractId,OutlineAgreement,"")))
| eval approval_threshold=tonumber(coalesce(region_threshold_usd,corp_po_threshold_usd,"25000"))
| eval auto_approved=tostring(coalesce(AutoApprovedFlag,AutomationTier,"false"))
| eval sourcing_event_id=tostring(coalesce(SourcingAwardId,RfxProjectKey,""))
| eval workflow_path=tostring(coalesce(RoutingChainSummary,ApprovalChainHash,"standard"))
| lookup workflow_allowlist.csv commodity_category OUTPUT allowed_workflow_paths
| eval maverick_category=case(
    preferred_flag IN ("false","0","no") AND (isnull(contract_id) OR contract_id=""),"off_contract",
    preferred_flag IN ("true","1","yes") AND isnotnull(cats_allowed_csv) AND NOT match(cats_allowed_csv, commodity_category),"off_catalog",
    po_amount_usd < approval_threshold AND match(lower(auto_approved),"true|yes|1") AND isnull(sourcing_event_id),"below_threshold_bypass",
    isnotnull(allowed_workflow_paths) AND NOT match(workflow_path,allowed_workflow_paths),"workflow_bypass",
    1=1,"compliant")
| eval negotiated_discount_pct=tonumber(coalesce(negotiated_discount_pct_csv,DistributorDiscountPct,"0"))/100
| eval savings_leakage_usd=if(maverick_category!="compliant", round(po_amount_usd*negotiated_discount_pct,2), 0)
| lookup supplier_risk.csv supplier_id OUTPUT dnb_risk_score AS dnb_score, ofac_sanctions_status AS ofac_stat, ecovadis_score AS eco_score
| eval high_risk_supplier_count=if(dnb_score>75 OR eco_score<45 OR match(lower(ofac_stat),"possible_match|review"),1,0)
| eval ofac_confirmed_hit=if(match(lower(ofac_stat),"confirmed_match"),1,0)
| stats avg(requisition_to_approval_days) AS avg_cycle_days perc50(requisition_to_approval_days) AS p50_cycle_days perc95(requisition_to_approval_days) AS p95_cycle_days sum(po_amount_usd) AS spend_usd sum(eval(if(maverick_category!="compliant",po_amount_usd,0))) AS maverick_spend_usd dc(po_id) AS po_cnt sum(eval(if(maverick_category!="compliant",1,0))) AS maverick_po_cnt sum(savings_leakage_usd) AS savings_leakage_usd sum(high_risk_supplier_count) AS high_risk_supplier_count sum(ofac_confirmed_hit) AS ofac_confirmed_hits BY legal_entity,cost_center,commodity_category
| eval maverick_pct=if(spend_usd>0, round(100*maverick_spend_usd/spend_usd,2), null())
| join max=1 type=left [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:invoice_match_log" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
    | stats sum(eval(if(lower(match_type)="auto_3way",1,0))) AS touchless_cnt count AS invoice_lines_total BY legal_entity,cost_center
    | eval touchless_rate=round(100*touchless_cnt/nullif(invoice_lines_total,0),2)
 ]
| join max=1 type=left [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:budget_bridge_qtd" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
    | stats latest(q_budget_usd) AS q_budget_usd BY cost_center
 ]
| fillnull value=0 touchless_rate savings_leakage_usd maverick_pct q_budget_usd
| eval budget_consumed_pct=if(q_budget_usd>0, round(100*spend_usd/nullif(q_budget_usd,0),1), null())
| streamstats current=t window=4 global=false sum(spend_usd) AS rolling_4w_spend BY commodity_category
| where maverick_pct>10 OR p95_cycle_days>21 OR savings_leakage_usd>50000 OR ofac_confirmed_hits>0
| sort - savings_leakage_usd
| table legal_entity,cost_center,commodity_category,avg_cycle_days,p50_cycle_days,p95_cycle_days,spend_usd,maverick_pct,maverick_spend_usd,savings_leakage_usd,touchless_rate,budget_consumed_pct,rolling_4w_spend,maverick_po_cnt,ofac_confirmed_hits
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio workbook layering trellised histograms of `requisition_to_approval_days` across legal entities; combo charts plotting rolling maverick percentages beside APQC formula references annotated text panels; bullet graphs contrasting median versus ninety-fifth percentile cycle times against Coupa BSM Index benchmarks cited inline; heatmaps pairing commodity categories with maverick dollar leakage and EcoVadis score tiers; Sankey sketches tying compliant versus maverick dollars through supplier tiers using Splunk Sankey custom viz optional; drill-through tables exposing representative `po_number`, masked hash surrogate columns (`requester_email_hash`), plus Splunk drill tokens linking back into Coupa PO workspaces through encoded HTTPS macros storing tenant hosts outside PAN scope.
